channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion. This expansion enhances the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to engage attendees early with innovative experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on sessions about agentic AI, offering developers insights into new technologies. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration in tech development.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which improves GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance on-device inference and security within the TensorFlow ecosystem.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. It focuses on practical applications of AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and custom commands. These tools aim to make AI a more effective coding collaborator.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce manual coding efforts. These tools help developers by providing context-aware assistance.

item.title: 30 years later, I returned to Enlightenment Linux to test the Elive beta - and it's much better
item.description: A review of the Enlightenment Linux Elive beta shows improvements in the old-school desktop environment, although some features are still lacking. The distro aims to modernize while retaining classic elements.

item.title: Growing void between enterprise and frontier AI puts open weights models in the spotlight
item.description: The demand for open weights AI models is increasing as enterprises seek affordable, effective solutions that protect proprietary data. Companies like Google and Microsoft are responding with new offerings.

item.title: Here's my favorite email trick for cleaning up inbox clutter - automatically
item.description: A simple email trick can help users automatically clean up their inboxes, reducing clutter from ads and newsletters. This feature is available in most email solutions.

item.title: Apple update looks like Czech mate for locked-out iPhone user
item.description: A university student in the US is unable to access his iPhone due to Apple removing a character from its Czech keyboard, which is part of his passcode. This incident highlights potential accessibility issues for users with non-standard characters in their passwords.

item.title: After using Lenovo's new Yoga laptop, I'm wondering if Windows makers are running out of ideas
item.description: Lenovo's new Yoga 7a 2-in-1 laptop offers versatility but struggles with mid-tier hardware that makes it less appealing compared to competitors. This raises questions about innovation in Windows laptop designs.

item.title: The $30 Google TV stick may be the budget Chromecast successor we've been waiting for
item.description: Walmart is set to release a new $30 Google TV stick, which may serve as an affordable successor to the Chromecast, appealing to budget-conscious streaming fans. This device could reshape the budget streaming market.

item.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman addressed concerns raised in a New Yorker article and responded to an attack on his home, emphasizing his commitment to transparency and trust. This response highlights the scrutiny faced by tech leaders.

item.title: How Salesforce and ServiceNow are squaring off in the battle for the helpdesk
item.description: Salesforce and ServiceNow are competing for dominance in the helpdesk market, with Salesforce focusing on user engagement while ServiceNow emphasizes AI governance. This rivalry could shape the future of IT service management.

item.title: I stopped guessing which AA batteries are dead - this charging station keeps them in check for me
item.description: A review praises the Olight Ostation 2 Pro battery charger for its ability to keep track of battery life, making it easier for users to manage their AA batteries. This product addresses a common frustration for many consumers.

item.title: Two different attackers poisoned popular open source tools - and showed us the future of supply chain compromise
item.description: Two supply chain attacks in March compromised popular open source tools, injecting malware and stealing sensitive information from numerous organizations. This incident underscores the vulnerabilities in software supply chains.

item.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
item.description: The New Yorker’s illustration of Sam Altman sparked reactions for its unsettling portrayal, highlighting the intersection of AI art and public perception of tech leaders. This raises questions about representation in media.

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: The FBI has access to push notifications, raising concerns about privacy and security for users. This revelation comes amid other significant news, including Iran's internet blackout and cryptocurrency scams.

item.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
item.description: A casual conversation reveals speculation about Mitski's father being a CIA operative, showcasing how rumors can spread in unexpected ways. This highlights the intersection of celebrity culture and conspiracy theories.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: The article discusses how the rise of AI-generated content and restricted data has compromised online verification systems, making it harder to discern truth from falsehoods. This highlights a growing challenge in maintaining trust in digital information.

item.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best augmented reality (AR) and mixed reality (MR) glasses available in 2026 showcases their capabilities in blending digital content with the real world for enhanced work and entertainment. This technology is poised to transform user experiences significantly.

item.title: How Iran out-shitposted the White House
item.description: The article contrasts the Iranian regime's effective use of social media during conflict with the White House's less impactful online presence. It illustrates how state media can dominate narratives in times of crisis.

item.title: Hungarian government creds left in the safe hands of 'FrankLampard'
item.description: Hungary's government faced a security breach exposing nearly 800 state logins, including sensitive defense accounts. This incident underscores vulnerabilities in password management and national security.

item.title: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description: GitHub Copilot is implementing new limits and retiring its Opus 4.6 Fast feature due to increased usage patterns. This move aims to manage demand and ensure platform integrity.

item.title: Snowflake manager explains the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI agent data access
item.description: Snowflake's director discusses the importance of clean and accessible data for AI agents, suggesting that data quality is a major bottleneck in AI development. This perspective emphasizes the need for better data governance.

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description: GitHub has renamed its Copilot coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and is updating its data schema to reflect this change. This adjustment aims to streamline user experience and data management.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: Anthropic has temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing Claude following a pricing change for users. This action raises questions about platform governance and user access.

item.title: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description: GitHub is pausing new trials for Copilot Pro due to significant abuse of the free trial system, aiming to protect legitimate users and maintain platform integrity.

item.title: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description: GitHub has implemented a limit of 50 reruns for Actions workflows, introducing a new restriction to manage system performance and user expectations.

item.title: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description: GitHub has integrated Copilot CLI activity into its usage metrics, allowing for a more comprehensive view of user engagement and feature performance. This update enhances the understanding of how users interact with the CLI tool.

item.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
item.description: A discount of 50% is being offered on the Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver, making it a cost-effective option for DIY enthusiasts. This deal is timely for those looking to tackle home repairs and projects.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI and robotics, highlighting their impact across various industries. These developments are crucial for the future of automation and efficiency.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, is raising concerns about cybersecurity, as it could empower malicious actors. Experts warn that developers must prioritize security in AI development to mitigate potential risks.

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot strategy is just more user abuse from Redmond, says Mozilla
item.description: Mozilla criticizes Microsoft's Copilot strategy, claiming it undermines user choice and reflects outdated web practices. The backlash follows Microsoft's decision to limit certain Copilot features in Windows.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: A stalking victim has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging that ChatGPT contributed to her abuser's delusions and failed to heed her warnings about his dangerous behavior. This case raises questions about AI's responsibility in user interactions.

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: An individual has been arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, following threats made outside the company's headquarters. This incident underscores rising tensions surrounding AI leaders.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been arrested for throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's residence, with the act captured on surveillance. This incident highlights security concerns for prominent figures in the tech industry.

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A beginner's guide to the GitHub Copilot CLI has been released, offering step-by-step instructions for new users. This resource aims to help users effectively utilize the tool for coding tasks.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description: GitHub's Copilot cloud agent has improved its validation tools, making them 20% faster in running security and quality checks on code. This enhancement aims to streamline the development process for users.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing aims to invest $100 million in AI resources to identify and fix vulnerabilities in open source software, using its Mythos AI program. This initiative highlights the dual-edged nature of AI in cybersecurity, as it can both discover and potentially create vulnerabilities.

item.title: Britain seeks views before it drops the hammer on signal jammers
item.description: The UK government is gathering public opinions on radiofrequency jammers as it prepares to legislate against these devices, which are often associated with criminal activities. This move indicates a tightening of regulations around technology that can disrupt communications.

item.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman's tumultuous leadership at OpenAI included a brief firing and subsequent reinstatement, leading to significant organizational changes. This period has raised questions about leadership stability and direction within the AI sector.

item.title: OpenAI Backs Bill That Would Limit Liability for AI-Enabled Mass Deaths or Financial Disasters
item.description: OpenAI has supported an Illinois bill that would limit its liability in cases of mass harm caused by AI technologies. This controversial stance raises ethical questions about accountability in AI development.

item.title: ACM Human-Computer Interaction Conference (CHI) 2026
item.description: Apple will present new research at the ACM CHI Conference 2026, focusing on human-computer interaction. This event is significant for showcasing advancements in technology and user experience.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly from security assessment results, providing contextual guidance for risk management. This aims to enhance security practices within organizations.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate ongoing tasks for businesses using AI. This development aims to streamline operations and reduce manual workloads.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta AI's app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store following the launch of its new model, Muse Spark, indicating strong user interest and engagement. This reflects the growing popularity of AI applications in consumer markets.

item.title: Microsoft named a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms
item.description: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Sovereign Cloud Platforms, reflecting its strong position in addressing evolving data sovereignty needs. This recognition underscores Microsoft's commitment to cloud solutions.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its Mythos AI model due to concerns about its ability to find security exploits, raising questions about whether this is a genuine cybersecurity measure or a protective strategy for the company.
